acceptance_criteria: sma.gen3.json validates against the module map in .UltraVision/modules.json
npm run sma:gen3 -- --changed-file tools/sma-lease.mjs prints lane single-module (coord)
description: Run `uvp gen3-draft` against .UltraVision/modules.json, review the emitted meta/sma.gen3.draft.json, adapt and commit as repo-root sma.gen3.json. Add npm scripts sma:gen3 / sma:gen3:json / sma:gen3:check (minimal classifier reading sma.gen3.json ownership globs and printing the lane for --changed-file). Anchor the lane rule in AGENTS.md.
title: Adopt sma.gen3.json from uvp gen3-draft

acceptance_criteria: templates render on GitHub
first-timer template links intro lane
description: .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/: bug.yml (repro + expected + got), feature.yml (problem-first), first-timer.yml ('I'm new and stuck' — explicitly welcomed, points at intro lane + SUPPORT). config.yml links Discussions. Warm microcopy, zero bureaucracy.
title: Issue + discussion templates for humans of every level

acceptance_criteria: tsc --noEmit exits 0 on the full tree
npm run check includes typecheck and stays green
no bare @ts-ignore anywhere (only @ts-expect-error with reason)
description: Create tsconfig.json at repo root: {allowJs:true, checkJs:true, noEmit:true, strict:false, module:'nodenext', moduleResolution:'nodenext', target:'es2022', include:['tools/**/*'], exclude:['node_modules']}. Add devDependency typescript (pinned ^5.x) and npm script "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit". Drive the existing .mjs tree to a clean pass: prefer real JSDoc fixes; @ts-expect-error with a reason comment only where a fix needs the file's future TS conversion. Wire typecheck into npm run check.
title: Author tsconfig + typecheck gate for the whole tree

acceptance_criteria: workflow green on a no-op PR
each of the four jobs fails the PR when its gate is broken (verified by one deliberate red run each)
description: Create .github/workflows/ci.yml (Node 24, ubuntu-latest) triggered on push to main + all PRs, jobs: (1) check — npm install && npm run check; (2) typecheck — npm run typecheck; (3) gitleaks — official gitleaks-action with .gitleaks.toml; (4) leaks — node tools/sma-leak-gate.mjs (depends on UV-GA-leak-gate-tool). Cache node_modules. Badge in README.
title: GitHub Actions CI: check + typecheck + gitleaks + leak gate

acceptance_criteria: tag push produces a release with 2 binaries + plugin tarball
description: Create .github/workflows/release.yml on v* tags: build smarch-core (cargo, linux-x64 + darwin-arm64 matrix) once rust-core exists, attach binaries to the GitHub release, npm pack the .claude-plugin bundle and attach. Changelog from conventional commits.
title: Release workflow: binaries + plugin bundle on tag
